match: Load ONLY the files listed for your task type. Do not load others.
content: Also run `search_memory(task description)` at session start — category filters are listed per task type.
· mem0: category:[pattern, constraint, integration]
· mem0: category:[gotcha, resolution, constraint]
· mem0: category:[pattern, constraint]
- CONSULT MEMORY: At session start, run `search_memory` with your task description using the category filter for your task type. Pull full content for confirmed-confidence results.
- CAPTURE TO MEMORY: After any task that produced a new pattern, gotcha, or resolution, follow `.agents/playbooks/memory-write.md`.
- `.agents/playbooks/memory-write.md` — memory write protocol (follow before calling add_memory)
- **mem0** — agent-discovered pattern, gotcha, or resolution
→ Applies when: learned during task execution, scoped to specific conditions, sub-ADR formality
→ Follow `.agents/playbooks/memory-write.md` — DO NOT call add_memory directly
→ Signals: unexpected system behavior, approach that worked under specific conditions, a watch-out
- ADR vs memory: learning changes HOW WE BUILD → ADR; learning records WHAT WAS DISCOVERED → memory
- principles/ vs memory: applies universally to all future tasks → principle; applies under specific conditions → memory

match: - Trace execution path to identify root cause
content: - Query mem0 for prior resolutions matching this symptom before investigating: `search_memory([bug description], filter={category: [gotcha, resolution, constraint]})`
## Step 5 — Capture Memory · act  (skip if no new learning)
Actions:
- Identify any gotcha, resolution, or constraint discovered during this task not already in memory
- Follow `.agents/playbooks/memory-write.md` for each candidate
Gate: all learnings captured or determined not memory-worthy
